title: Bart Gets Hit By A Car

jokes.text: Just give him a nickel and let's get going!
jokes.setup: Someone suggests the boy is hurt and needs help
jokes.punchline: Burns' absurdly callous response - a nickel as medical compensation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Burns' miserly nature turned up to absurd levels. Economy of words with maximum character revelation.

jokes.text: Hey, cool. I'm dead!
jokes.setup: Bart has apparently died in the accident
jokes.punchline: His casual, excited reaction to his own death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - three words that perfectly capture Bart's irreverent personality. The casual excitement about death is genuinely funny and character-specific.

jokes.text: Please hold on to the handrail. Do not spit over the side.
jokes.setup: Bart is presumably on an escalator to the afterlife
jokes.punchline: Mundane safety instructions for the journey to eternity
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of the profound (afterlife) with the mundane (safety instructions). Good concept execution.

jokes.text: Snowball! Do not spit over the side.
jokes.setup: Bart sees his dead cat Snowball in the afterlife
jokes.punchline: The guide continues the spitting prohibition even during an emotional reunion
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that maintains the absurd bureaucracy gag. Not groundbreaking but functional.

jokes.text: I'm Bart Simpson. Who the hell are you? Please allow me to introduce myself. I'm the devil!
jokes.setup: Polite introduction exchange in what appears to be Hell
jokes.punchline: The casual reveal that he's talking to Satan himself
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Bart's irreverence and the Devil's politeness. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: Spitting just clinched it.
jokes.setup: Devil explains Bart's damnation for evil deeds
jokes.punchline: The trivial act of spitting was the final straw for eternal punishment
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the spitting setup. The absurdity of minor infractions leading to damnation is well-executed.

jokes.text: It's a mistake. You're due to arrive when the Yankees win the pennant. Nearly a century from now. Boy, is my face red!
jokes.setup: Devil checks Bart's file for his scheduled death
jokes.punchline: The implication that the Yankees won't win for nearly a century, plus casual embarrassment about the mix-up
jokes.explanation: Multi-layered joke: Yankees futility, bureaucratic Hell, and casual tone about eternal damnation. Strong sports reference that ages well.

jokes.text: Remember, lie, cheat, steal and listen to heavy metal music!
jokes.setup: Devil gives Bart advice on avoiding Hell
jokes.punchline: Three serious sins followed by listening to heavy metal as equally damning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with the heavy metal punchline. Plays on moral panic themes of the era.

jokes.text: And you were there! And you. And you. And you. You I've never seen before.
jokes.setup: Bart doing the classic 'Wizard of Oz' everyone-was-in-my-dream bit
jokes.punchline: Breaks the pattern with a stranger he doesn't recognize
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the Wizard of Oz trope. Good timing and structure in the delivery.

jokes.text: It turns into a sponge in water.
jokes.setup: Hutz hands out his business card
jokes.punchline: His card has this completely impractical feature
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - shows Hutz's incompetence through a specific, absurd business choice. Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: How does a nice girl know that word?
jokes.setup: Marge asks if he's a shyster
jokes.punchline: Hutz is surprised she knows the word rather than denying he is one
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - we expect a denial, get concern about her vocabulary. Shows Hutz's twisted priorities perfectly.

jokes.text: Is that a broken neck? Great!
jokes.setup: Hutz notices someone's potential injury
jokes.punchline: His excited response to what should be tragic
jokes.explanation: Perfect ambulance-chasing lawyer characterization. The enthusiasm about injury is darkly funny and very specific to his character.

jokes.text: We've got a nasty bump on our head. Quit it! And a tiny broken toe. Quit it!
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert uses 'we' to describe Bart's injuries
jokes.punchline: Bart's repeated objection to the collective pronoun
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment - Bart's independence versus medical paternalism. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Simpson, at last we meet.
jokes.setup: Burns dramatically greets his employee
jokes.punchline: They work together - this isn't their first meeting
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Burns' dramatic flair combined with his complete disconnection from his employees.

jokes.text: So extortion is the name of your game?
jokes.setup: Homer mentions medical bills after Burns offers $100
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately jumps to calling it extortion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns escalation - reasonable request becomes criminal conspiracy in his mind. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Tangle with me, and I'll crush you like a paper cup.
jokes.setup: Burns threatens Homer with his lawyers
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and somewhat weak simile for crushing
jokes.explanation: The paper cup simile is unexpectedly specific and somewhat anticlimactic, which fits Burns' dramatic but out-of-touch character.

jokes.text: You don't have to do that. I can throw myself out.
jokes.setup: Burns orders Smithers to throw Homer out
jokes.punchline: Homer's helpful offer to save them the trouble
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - helpful even in humiliation. Shows his oblivious nature and people-pleasing tendency.

jokes.text: The Supreme Court called. They need your help on a freedom thing. Tell them to sit tight. I'll get back to them.
jokes.setup: Hutz's secretary announces important calls
jokes.punchline: The Supreme Court needs help with 'a freedom thing' and Hutz casually postpones them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdity - vague legal terminology ('freedom thing') plus the casual dismissal of the highest court. Perfect Hutz characterization.

jokes.text: Yes, Harvard, Yale, MIT, Oxford, the Sorbonne, the Louvre.
jokes.setup: Homer compliments Hutz's education credentials
jokes.punchline: List starts plausibly but ends with an art museum
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that reveals Hutz's fraudulent nature. The Louvre inclusion is perfectly absurd and character-revealing.

jokes.text: But just between you and me, I promise you a big cash settlement.
jokes.setup: Hutz says the bar forbids him from promising a settlement
jokes.punchline: Immediately promises exactly that
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Hutz's unethical practice. The immediate contradiction is well-timed.

jokes.text: You'll also get this faux pearl necklace, a $99 value, as our gift.
jokes.setup: Hutz explains his 50% fee arrangement
jokes.punchline: Adds a cheesy infomercial-style bonus gift
jokes.explanation: Good satire of sleazy advertising tactics applied to legal services. The 'faux' pearl detail is perfect.

jokes.text: You see that dark spot there? Whiplash.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick examines Bart's X-rays
jokes.punchline: Diagnoses whiplash from a random dark spot
jokes.explanation: Good establishment of Dr. Nick's fraudulent medical practice. Simple but effective incompetence gag.

jokes.text: Am I going to die? Yes, son!
jokes.setup: Bart asks if he'll die
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick's casual, enthusiastic confirmation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with inappropriate enthusiasm. Perfect Dr. Nick bedside manner parody.

jokes.text: Will I ever play baseball again? No!
jokes.setup: Classic 'will I play again' medical question
jokes.punchline: Immediate negative without examination
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of medical drama trope. Dr. Nick's instant pessimism is character-consistent.

jokes.text: The only person here who comes close is him. Stop, you're embarrassing me!
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick says no one present is a real doctor
jokes.punchline: Points to Bart, who's fake-embarrassed by the 'compliment'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdity with Bart's mock embarrassment. Shows his enjoyment of the con game.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Tons of it. Just say when.
jokes.setup: Hutz asks about soft tissue trauma
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick's eager confirmation with unlimited supply
jokes.explanation: Perfect willing accomplice characterization. The 'just say when' is like ordering deli meat.

jokes.text: Let him twist in the wind. Slowly, slowly. When the papers have found their new flavor of the month, he'll find out this cat has claws!
jokes.setup: Burns decides to wait before firing Homer
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphorical threat ending with 'this cat has claws'
jokes.explanation: Good Burns villainy with the cat metaphor being appropriately theatrical and slightly ridiculous.

jokes.text: But what is truth, if you follow me?
jokes.setup: Lisa points out Bart would have to tell the truth in court
jokes.punchline: Hutz's philosophical deflection of basic legal ethics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Hutz turns basic honesty into philosophical debate. Perfect lawyer parody.

jokes.text: You mean like I'm dead? Sure. The kid's a pro.
jokes.setup: Hutz asks Bart to roll his eyes back
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual interpretation as looking dead, Hutz's approval
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Bart's natural talent for deception and Hutz's appreciation for it.

jokes.text: I should be able to run over as many kids as I want!
jokes.setup: Burns' lawyer reminds him of his wealth and status
jokes.punchline: Burns' absurd interpretation of his privileges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns entitlement taken to absurd extreme. Dark humor that perfectly captures his character's moral void.

jokes.text: You wouldn't dare! Well, no, I guess I wouldn't.
jokes.setup: Judge threatens Burns with contempt
jokes.punchline: Judge immediately backs down when challenged
jokes.explanation: Great power dynamic comedy. The immediate capitulation perfectly shows Burns' influence.

jokes.text: I was about to be struck by the luxury car of death!
jokes.setup: Bart describes the accident in dramatic terms
jokes.punchline: Burns' car becomes 'the luxury car of death'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant phrase that's both dramatic and specific. 'Luxury car of death' is highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Although sometimes I wish I had been.
jokes.setup: Bart says luckily he wasn't killed
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts with death wish
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor and perfect timing for the contradiction. Shows Bart's theatrical nature.

jokes.text: I was driving to the orphanage to pass out toys.
jokes.setup: Burns gives his version of events
jokes.punchline: Claims he was doing charity work - completely out of character
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Burns claiming charitable motives is perfectly absurd for his character.

jokes.text: You cold-blooded monster!
jokes.setup: Burns continues his fictional testimony
jokes.punchline: He calls himself a cold-blooded monster in his own story
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Burns accidentally reveals his true nature even in his fabricated version. Self-awareness without self-reflection.

jokes.text: Take me, I'm old!
jokes.setup: Burns continues his charitable fantasy
jokes.punchline: Dramatic self-sacrifice offer that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Burns moment - dramatic gesture that doesn't make logical sense but fits his theatrical nature.

jokes.text: What trial were you watching?
jokes.setup: Hutz says it's looking good after obvious disaster
jokes.punchline: Homer's incredulous response
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Homer's occasional moments of clarity versus Hutz's delusion.

jokes.text: Just get that ape to my house and we'll buy him off with a banana!
jokes.setup: Burns agrees to settlement negotiations
jokes.punchline: Reduces Homer to an ape who can be bought with fruit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns contempt for his workers. The banana detail is absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: I don't like everything about them. Just their heads.
jokes.setup: Homer says he didn't know Burns liked animals
jokes.punchline: Burns clarifies he only likes their severed heads as trophies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that perfectly captures Burns' twisted nature. Great misdirection and character revelation.

jokes.text: He's offering us this because he knows he'll lose and have to pay us a million.
jokes.setup: Hutz explains why they should reject the settlement
jokes.punchline: His logic after Homer just sabotaged their case
jokes.explanation: Good Hutz delusion - complete disconnect from reality of their destroyed case. Character-consistent incompetence.

jokes.text: I spit on his $500,000!
jokes.setup: Hutz rejects Burns' settlement offer
jokes.punchline: Spitting reference connects back to Bart's hell-damning escalator violation
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback to the spitting that damned Bart. The irony of spitting on money while pursuing a lawsuit adds character depth.

jokes.setup: Hutz rejects Burns' offer dramatically
jokes.punchline: Spits on an enormous sum of money
jokes.explanation: Good greed escalation. The dramatic rejection of a fortune shows Hutz's poor judgment and gambling addiction.

jokes.text: Smithers, release the hounds.
jokes.setup: Negotiations have broken down
jokes.punchline: Burns' signature threat/catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic Burns catchphrase delivered at perfect moment. Highly quotable and character-defining.

jokes.text: Oh, the truth!
jokes.setup: Marge takes oath seriously
jokes.punchline: Hutz's dismayed reaction to honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect lawyer parody - truth as the enemy. Good economy and character revelation.

jokes.text: My mother said, 'If you can't say anything nice, don't say anything.' Will that hold up? No, I've tried.
jokes.setup: Marge quotes wholesome advice about not saying mean things
jokes.punchline: Hutz has tested this legal strategy and failed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant humor - Hutz has actually tried using wholesome sayings as legal defense. Great character moment and original concept.

jokes.text: And he mispronounced words like 'abdomen.'
jokes.setup: Marge describes Dr. Nick's incompetence
jokes.punchline: Specific example of mispronouncing basic medical terms
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that builds the incompetence case. Marge's careful observation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, we can't blame them for trying, can we?
jokes.setup: Judge points out the million-dollar discrepancy
jokes.punchline: Marge's gentle, understanding response to obvious fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge character moment - finds the good even in obvious legal fraud. Her kindness destroys their case beautifully.

jokes.text: A million dollars. My wife cost me a million dollars.
jokes.setup: Homer sees the reduced settlement offer
jokes.punchline: Blames Marge for his loss rather than his own actions
jokes.explanation: Good Homer blame-shifting. Ignores that his outburst ruined the case, focuses anger on Marge's honesty.

jokes.text: A million dollars' worth, you snake-woman! No. String beans? No, you two-timing, backstabbing-- Better answer. No.
jokes.setup: Homer is furious at Marge during dinner
jokes.punchline: Catches himself mid-insult and forces politeness
jokes.explanation: Good internal struggle comedy - Homer fighting his anger with forced politeness. The interruption of his insult is well-timed.

jokes.text: You're better off. Rich people aren't happy. They think they're happy but trust me, they ain't.
jokes.setup: Homer laments his lost wealth
jokes.punchline: Barney's dubious consolation about rich people's unhappiness
jokes.explanation: Good barfly wisdom. Barney's confident assertion about rich people's psychology is absurd coming from his position.

jokes.text: Homer, I'd like you to forgive me for doing the right thing.
jokes.setup: Marge comes to reconcile with Homer
jokes.punchline: Asks forgiveness for her integrity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that captures the absurdity of the situation. Marge apologizing for honesty is both funny and touching. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: She's your wife of 10 years, you've had 3 children. It's time to be honest.
jokes.setup: Homer considers whether he loves Marge anymore
jokes.punchline: Random drunk gives marriage advice
jokes.explanation: Decent bar wisdom humor. The drunk offering sound relationship advice is a nice role reversal.

jokes.text: I'll still do the bed stuff.
jokes.setup: Homer says he might not love Marge anymore
jokes.punchline: Assures her their physical relationship will continue
jokes.explanation: Good Homer crudeness disguised as consideration. The euphemism 'bed stuff' is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Do it if it'll shut her up. Start with the feet. Still angry. Good, Homer! This is tough, need refreshment. Trustworthy beer.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to look at Marge and find love
jokes.punchline: His stream-of-consciousness internal commentary
jokes.explanation: Excellent internal monologue humor. Homer's process of rediscovering love is both crude and sweet. 'Trustworthy beer' is perfect.

jokes.text: For the next 15 minutes, one-third off on every pitcher. One per customer, domestic beer only. Hey, no sharing!
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge reconcile romantically
jokes.punchline: Moe immediately turns it into a promotional opportunity with restrictions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - turns romantic reconciliation into business opportunity. The increasing restrictions make it funnier.
